title: Verbascum mairei
intro: Verbascum mairei, also known as Maire's mullein or Verbascum mairei, is a species of flowering plant in the mullein genus, characterized by its tall stature and large, showy flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties, and its health benefits also include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Verbascum mairei include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Verbascum mairei is commonly used in herbal medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being included in topical creams and ointments for skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Verbascum mairei is classified within the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the figwort family. This family consists of flowering plants, many of which are found in Europe. Verbascum mairei is further classified under the genus Verbascum, which contains around 150 species. The genus Verbascum is native to the Northern Hemisphere and is characterized by its tall, upright spikes of small flowers. Verbascum mairei is a type of mullein plant, which is known for its large, velvety leaves and tall flower stalks.
